papers.title: Terminus-4B: Can a Smaller Model Replace Frontier LLMs at Agentic Execution Tasks?
papers.innovation: Proposes an architectural pattern of delegating specialized subtasks to subagents to manage context and improve efficiency, demonstrating that smaller models (Terminus-4B) can perform effectively on agentic execution tasks by isolating verbose outputs and reducing memory pressures.
papers.benchmarks: Evaluates performance on agentic execution tasks such as search, debugging, and terminal execution, implicitly comparing the efficiency and capability of smaller models against larger frontier LLMs.
papers.use_case: Developing cost-effective and efficient agentic AI systems, optimizing LLM context window usage, designing modular multi-agent architectures, and leveraging specialized subagents for specific responsibilities to enhance scalability and performance.

papers.title: Learning Correct Behavior from Examples: Validating Sequential Execution in Autonomous Agents
papers.innovation: Introduces a novel algorithm that automatically learns correct sequential behavior from a few examples and validates agent executions, significantly reducing the need for manual specification, exact sequence matching, or thousands of training examples for testing autonomous agents.
papers.benchmarks: Focuses on validating sequential execution behavior in autonomous agents, addressing limitations of traditional testing methods that require extensive manual effort or large datasets.
papers.use_case: Automated testing and validation of complex autonomous agents, ensuring reliability and adherence to desired operational sequences, and streamlining the development and quality assurance processes for agentic systems.

papers.title: Stable Agentic Control: Tool-Mediated LLM Architecture for Autonomous Cyber Defense
papers.innovation: Proposes a tool-mediated LLM architecture designed to provide formal guarantees for agentic systems operating in high-stakes, adversarial environments, specifically for autonomous cyber defense, addressing the critical need for reliability and stability in such applications.
papers.benchmarks: Aims to meet the operational needs of security operations centers (SOCs) for configuring endpoint detection and response (EDR) policies under adversarial pressure, implying validation against security-critical scenarios and formal properties.
papers.use_case: Designing and deploying robust, verifiable, and secure AI agents for critical applications like cybersecurity, industrial control systems, and other domains requiring high-stakes, reliable decision-making under adversarial conditions.
